title: Approve one bounded worktree-path reconciliation so normal runner implementation can proceed?
description:
Beads-backed approval request.

Question: Approve one bounded worktree-path reconciliation so normal runner implementation can proceed?

Context:
Normal-mode diagnostic pi-2m1.38.15 proved the bootstrap worktree was created with dotted path/branch `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1.38` / `epic/pi-2m1.38`, while the existing policy deterministically slugifies epicId pi-2m1.38 to `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1-38` / `epic/pi-2m1-38`. The registered worktree is clean at checkpoint 673c348. Metadata has no path override, so every implementation task is truthfully blocked. The smallest correction is to move that clean worktree and rename its local branch to the established resolver convention. There is no structured safe worktree-move gateway, so execution requires either one bounded repair-tools session or manual commands. No source implementation occurs in this step.

Options:
- Approve bounded repair-tools worktree move and local branch rename
- I prefer to run reviewed manual commands
- Reject and redesign the resolver instead

Requested default: Approve bounded repair-tools worktree move and local branch rename
Requesting run: pi-2m1.38-worktree-path-reconciliation
Target bead: pi-2m1.38.14

- Action: Move the clean local worktree from `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1.38` to `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1-38` and rename local branch `epic/pi-2m1.38` to `epic/pi-2m1-38`
- Scope: Only Git worktree registration/path and the one local branch name for the clean dedicated harness worktree at HEAD 673c348. Record before/after worktree-list, HEAD, branch, and status evidence. Excludes file/source edits, commits, reset, clean, rebase, amend, fetch, push, merge, branch/worktree deletion, deployment, runner restart/force-stop, hooks, launch agents, secrets, Edabit worktree, and any other branch/worktree.
- Consequences: Existing Beads with epicId pi-2m1.38 will resolve to the moved worktree through current policy and normal implementation can dispatch. Human-readable artifacts mentioning the old dotted path remain historical evidence; no history is rewritten.
- Reversibility: A separately approved inverse local worktree move/branch rename can restore the old names. No remote branch or commit history changes.
- Closeout path: Enter bounded repair-tools mode only for this Git metadata operation; verify exact clean before/after state and policy resolution; resolve stale worktree blocker pi-53v0 without adding authority; disable repair-tools/reload normal mode; then let approved plan-only task pi-2m1.38.14 dispatch autonomously.

Implementation will be decomposed into bounded Beads under epic pi-2m1.38, run only through the normal autonomous runner in .worktrees/epic/pi-2m1.38, and use exact per-task writeSets with RED/GREEN, review, docs, and fresh verification. Already-completed P0 fixes will be reconciled and verified rather than reimplemented. The chosen concurrency direction is serial/simple. Budget implementation remains blocked until the user's custom amount is known.

- Approve all scoped source/test/docs recommendations plus verified local checkpoint commits on epic/pi-2m1.38

title: You selected a custom budget policy. Which concrete boundary should I implement?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: You selected a custom budget policy. Which concrete boundary should I implement?

Context:
A hard budget needs an explicit numeric and reset scope. Provider-reported cost is authoritative only after a worker ends, so any per-worker amount is a planning/escalation threshold rather than a mid-stream kill switch; the hard gate applies before the next dispatch. The architecture remediation may involve many bounded workers, so the earlier $25 total may be too restrictive.

Options:
- $100 per epic hard cap; warn/escalate before workers projected above $5
- $50 per epic hard cap; warn/escalate before workers projected above $3
- No per-worker threshold; $100 per epic hard cap only
- I will specify a different exact policy and amounts in chat

Requested default: $100 per epic hard cap; warn/escalate before workers projected above $5
Requesting run: architecture-remediation-budget-20260710
Target bead: pi-2m1.38.2

Approval preview:
- Action: Set the concrete fail-closed budget and escalation thresholds
- Scope: Determines scheduler cost gates and pi-2m1.38's initial budget. Does not dispatch work or spend money.
- Consequences: The runner accumulates actual provider cost after each run and refuses the next dispatch when the epic cap is missing, unknown, or exhausted. A per-worker threshold triggers an approval/escalation before unusually expensive planned work but cannot terminate an already-running provider call.
- Reversibility: Amounts can be raised, lowered, or reset through a later recorded human decision; accrued cost evidence is retained.
- Closeout path: Persist the policy in the remediation plan and Bead metadata; implement deterministic accounting/gate tests and a bounded normal-mode witness.
notes:
Beads-backed question resolved as answered: Answered in Pi UI: I will specify a different exact policy and amounts in chat
Beads-backed question resolved as answered: Budget policy: make limits configurable rather than hard-coded; estimates must be model-, provider-, and subscription-aware. First inspect the existing Pi extensions and any simple usage/subscription endpoint before choosing the accounting adapter. Track authoritative billed/marginal cost separately from retail-equivalent estimates when available. Seed nominally high limits during rollout so the new gate observes and reports without prematurely blocking normal work; tighten values later from evidence.
Beads-backed question resolved as answered: Concrete rollout policy after installed-source research pi-2m1.38.3: configurable per-epic dual ledger with nominal defaults marginalUsd=$250, retailEquivalentUsd=$1,000, and maxPlannedRunRetailUsd=$50. OpenAI Codex OAuth uses subscription_allowance with configured marginalUsd=0 while retaining catalog-derived retailEquivalentUsd; Anthropic/OpenRouter use catalog-derived marginal and retail estimates unless an authoritative charge becomes available; locally owned models use configured cash marginalUsd=0. Unknown provider policy, missing required usage/cost signals, known exhausted allowance, or a reached limit blocks the next dispatch with auditable evidence. Limits and provider policies are configuration, not hard-coded constants, and can be revised later.

title: Which fail-closed budget policy should autonomous scheduling use?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: Which fail-closed budget policy should autonomous scheduling use?

Context:
The review confirmed budget enforcement is currently a placeholder: spentUsd is not accumulated and auto-scheduling does not require a real limit. The remediation will record provider-reported cost after every invocation and stop before subsequent dispatch when the limit is missing, unknown, or exhausted. Because provider cost is only authoritative after a run, timeout and per-run model policy remain the in-flight bound. Current project runner telemetry shows roughly $6.93 spent during the stabilization campaign.

Options:
- Per-epic hard cap: require each autonomous epic to declare a total USD cap; use $25 for pi-2m1.38 and a $2 per-worker planning ceiling
- Rolling 24-hour project cap: $25 across all autonomous work plus a $2 per-worker planning ceiling
- Custom policy or amounts

Requested default: Per-epic hard cap: require each autonomous epic to declare a total USD cap; use $25 for pi-2m1.38 and a $2 per-worker planning ceiling
Requesting run: architecture-remediation-design-20260710
Target bead: pi-2m1.38.2

Approval preview:
- Action: Choose the persistent cost-budget boundary for autonomous runner dispatch
- Scope: Affects runner protocol/state, scheduler pre-dispatch gates, metrics accumulation, doctor/health reporting, tests, and documentation. Does not spend money or dispatch work by itself.
- Consequences: Per-epic caps align cost with approved work and require every autonomous epic to carry a budget. A rolling project cap is simpler for operators but unrelated epics compete for one allowance. Both fail closed when cost data or limits are unavailable and stop before the next worker, not mid-invocation.
- Reversibility: Budget amounts and policy can be changed through an audited human decision. Historical cost records remain immutable evidence.
- Closeout path: Record the selected policy and amounts in Beads/plan; implement accounting and pre-dispatch gates with deterministic tests and a bounded live witness before unattended operation.

title: Which runner execution model should the architecture remediation implement?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: Which runner execution model should the architecture remediation implement?

Context:
full-architecture-review.md found that current concurrency knobs and slot/conflict accounting imply parallelism, but worker execution is synchronous and only one in-process run actually proceeds. The review requires choosing either a documented serial runner with inert concurrency machinery removed, or genuine asynchronous multi-worker dispatch. The user's stated preference is a simple base architecture with clear state ownership and minimal corner cases.

Options:
- Serial and simple now: one worker at a time, remove inert concurrency machinery; design an async extension seam for later
- Implement true asynchronous multi-worker dispatch now
- Stage serial simplification first, then require a separate design/approval before async work

Requested default: Serial and simple now: one worker at a time, remove inert concurrency machinery; design an async extension seam for later
Requesting run: architecture-remediation-design-20260710
Target bead: pi-2m1.38.2

Approval preview:
- Action: Choose the runner concurrency architecture for P1/P2 remediation
- Scope: Affects runner scheduler/service/state design, tests, docs, and removal or implementation of concurrency controls. Does not itself edit source, start workers, deploy, or change Git history.
- Consequences: Serial/simple removes accidental complexity and narrows state transitions but does not run multiple workers concurrently. True async adds process supervision, concurrent state transitions, write-set arbitration, cancellation, and higher test burden now.
- Reversibility: Serial design can later gain async behind an explicit process-supervision interface. Converting async back to serial would require broader removal work.
- Closeout path: Record the choice in Beads and the architecture remediation plan; derive staged implementation and verification invariants before dispatch.

title: Which path should replace the repeated repair-mode workflow for remaining harness stabilization?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: Which path should replace the repeated repair-mode workflow for remaining harness stabilization?

Context:
The normal runner can complete application work, but harness self-repair still depends on temporary repair mode because verified harness changes are uncommitted in tracked main while implementation dispatch is restricted to approved epic worktrees. This means the user-defined final normal-path success criterion is not yet met. pi-2m1.27.87 is approved in design but should remain blocked until this bootstrap gap is resolved or the user explicitly chooses to continue repair mode.

Options:
- Recommended: stop repair mode and authorize a separately scoped one-time audited checkpoint into a dedicated harness epic worktree, then use normal runner only
- Continue temporary repair mode for pi-2m1.27.87 despite the known bootstrap gap
- Design a new dedicated config/main-worktree dispatch policy before further harness implementation

Requested default: Recommended: stop repair mode and authorize a separately scoped one-time audited checkpoint into a dedicated harness epic worktree, then use normal runner only
Requesting run: pi-2m1.27.87-self-hosting-decision
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.87

Approval preview:
- Action: Choose the durable execution path for remaining harness stabilization
- Scope: Decision only. It does not authorize a Git commit, branch/worktree creation, source edits, deployment, restart, merge, push, reset, deletion, or repair-mode use. Any chosen consequential execution receives a separate scoped approval.
- Consequences: The recommended path adds one audited bootstrap checkpoint but removes repeated repair-mode dependence afterward. Continuing repair mode is faster now but confirms the workflow remains incomplete. A new dispatch policy may be cleaner long-term but requires another bootstrap implementation first.
- Reversibility: The decision can be superseded before execution; no repository or runtime state changes occur from answering it.
- Closeout path: Record the chosen option, create a scoped implementation/approval plan, and keep pi-2m1.27.87 blocked until its execution path is normal-runner-capable or repair mode is explicitly chosen.

title: Approve the focused canonical terminal-artifact finalizer design and direct repair-mode implementation?
description:
Beads-backed approval request.

Question: Approve the focused canonical terminal-artifact finalizer design and direct repair-mode implementation?

Context:
Fresh normal-mode witnesses proved immutable provenance is fixed, but also reconfirmed unstable model-prefixed-only terminal artifacts, generic handoff overwrite, and empty closeout/health checks. Three approaches were considered: (A) add canonical copies inline in invoke_run_bundle—smallest but preserves duplicated terminal logic and overwrite defects; (B) recommended: introduce one tested `finalize_invocation_artifacts` path in runs.py that atomically writes canonical response/stderr/metrics, optionally writes compatibility aliases, preserves or appends to worker-authored handoff/lessons, emits structured artifact/transport/semantic checks, and feeds update_run_result once; (C) redesign all six terminal state writers now—architecturally strongest but too broad and high-risk for this slice. The recommended design advances Stage H's single-finalizer architecture without combining event streaming, typed tool policy, retry/requeue, projectRoot/workspaceRoot, or single transition ownership. Successful runs must have passing canonical-artifact/transport/semantic checks; failed/timeout runs preserve precise failed checks and evidence. If metrics are disabled/unavailable, canonical metrics.json records explicit unavailable state rather than disappearing. Existing model-prefixed files remain compatibility copies for old consumers.

Options:
- Approve recommended focused finalizer design and scoped direct implementation
- Request design changes
- Reject

Requested default: Approve recommended focused finalizer design and scoped direct implementation
Requesting run: pi-2m1.27.87-canonical-finalizer-design
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.87

Approval preview:
- Action: Implement the approved canonical terminal-artifact finalizer with behavioral TDD in temporary orchestrator repair-tools mode
- Scope: Only pi/agent/bin/agnt_lib/runs.py, tests/test_agnt.py, tests/test_runner.py, and docs/RUNNER-SERVICE.md if user-visible artifact paths require documentation, plus Beads/test evidence. No deployment, runner restart beyond entering repair mode, E2E source/worktree edits, Git commit/reset/push/merge, branch/worktree deletion, event-stream redesign, task/role policy changes, typed tool-policy changes, retry/requeue changes, hooks, launch agents, unrelated runtime cleanup, or secrets.
- Consequences: New run bundles will always expose stable canonical response/stderr/metrics paths, preserve richer handoff/lessons, and record real terminal artifact/transport/semantic checks. Model-prefixed compatibility files may remain. Live behavior does not change until separately approved deployment.
- Reversibility: Revert the scoped tracked diff before deployment; existing run bundles remain untouched and compatibility aliases limit consumer breakage.
- Closeout path: Behavioral RED/GREEN for OK, semantic ERROR/ambiguous, transport failure, timeout, metrics-disabled, and existing handoff; focused/full pytest, Ruff F821, routing/role smokes, config/shell/diff checks, independent review; then request separate deployment and normal-mode live-witness approval.

Question: Approve direct temporary-repair-tools implementation of the P0 semantic terminal outcome contract?

Context:
Fresh Stages D and G explicitly returned ERROR with evidence, but process exit 0 caused succeeded result/live/handoff state and auto-closure. Stage H found six terminal writers and recommends a single semantic finalization path. This first migration slice will add a deterministic line-level terminal marker parser and ensure explicit ERROR, missing, or ambiguous markers cannot become success; OK remains success. It will preserve full response evidence and prevent erroneous read-only auto-close. The existing scheduler blocker on pi-2m1.27.77 reflects intentionally non-dispatchable direct-repair metadata and grants no authority.

Options:
- Approve scoped semantic terminal repair
- Reject

Requested default: Approve scoped semantic terminal repair
Requesting run: p0-semantic-terminal-repair-20260710
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.77

Approval preview:
- Action: Direct RED/GREEN repair of worker semantic terminal outcome handling in temporary repair-tools mode
- Scope: Only pi/agent/bin/agnt_lib/invoke.py, runs.py, runner.py, runner_scheduler.py, tests/test_agnt.py, tests/test_runner.py, tests/test_runner_scheduler.py, and Beads/run evidence. Prefer fewer files if root cause is narrower. No E2E artifact edits, epic-worktree changes, deployment/restart, checkpoint/reset/commit, push/merge, worktree deletion, hooks, launch agents, or secrets.
- Consequences: Worker reports with explicit ERROR or invalid terminal markers will become failed outcomes even if the Pi process exits 0, preventing false success and auto-close. Existing successful OK behavior must remain.
- Reversibility: Revert the scoped source/test changes before deployment; live config remains unchanged until separately approved.
- Closeout path: Behavioral RED/GREEN, focused/full tests and smokes, separate deployment approval, normal-mode live ERROR and OK witnesses, then proceed to provenance/tool-policy slices.
notes: Beads-backed approval resolved as approved: Approved in Pi UI
close_reason: Beads-backed approval resolved as approved: Approved in Pi UI

Quality-budget review selects Sol; cheap/balanced defaults remain unchanged. This does not demote Gemma capability and does not alter worker tool permissions.

title: Hold the DAG auto-advance repair until architecture-review P0 safety prerequisites are resolved?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: Hold the DAG auto-advance repair until architecture-review P0 safety prerequisites are resolved?

Context:
I independently confirmed the four headline findings in full-architecture-review.md against current source: runner-service and scheduler use separate unlocked state write paths; drain completion is not re-evaluated after an active tick completes; gateway/approval resolution can accept model-supplied approved outcomes and gateway create_draft forwards metadata.pi.approved; benchmark/eval/prompt code reference missing imports. Because pi-2m1.27.44 would exercise more unattended write-worker operation, the review recommends treating these as prerequisites. This blocker prevents that task from dispatching; it does not pause the whole daemon or modify source.

Options:
- Hold pi-2m1.27.44 pending P0 safety remediation
- Allow pi-2m1.27.44 to proceed before P0 remediation

Requested default: Hold pi-2m1.27.44 pending P0 safety remediation
Requesting run: unknown
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.44

Approval preview:
- Action: Temporarily hold one queued unattended-runner repair
- Scope: Only pi-2m1.27.44 dispatch eligibility; no daemon stop, source edit, worktree action, or history change.
- Consequences: Prevents the queued DAG repair from using the current unsafe approval/state machinery until the P0 issues are addressed or explicitly waived.
- Reversibility: Resolve the decision to allow the task or supersede it after P0 fixes; this does not alter the bead’s contents.
- Closeout path: Create tracked P0 remediation work, verify it, then resolve this hold with evidence or an explicit waiver.

title: How should the DAG repair obtain a clean writable implementation worktree while the fresh E2E artifact remains intentionally dirty in the shared epic worktree?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: How should the DAG repair obtain a clean writable implementation worktree while the fresh E2E artifact remains intentionally dirty in the shared epic worktree?

Context:
The approved repair changes only runner/gateway files, but the existing required epic worktree .worktrees/epic/pi-2m1 is dirty with the fresh Stage A/B e2e-test/ artifact. Current policy correctly rejects an implementation that stages only pi/ files while unrelated e2e-test/ changes are dirty. The user-approved checkpoint policy pi-5eu permits a local evidenced checkpoint of approved E2E stages; pi-rbm prohibits reset, merge, push, deletion, or E2E edits. This needs an explicit worktree-handling choice before dispatch.

Options:
- Create an evidenced local checkpoint of the current e2e-test/ artifact, then repair in the same epic worktree
- Create a separate dedicated repair epic/worktree (requires a later worktree-creation approval)
- Wait until the fresh E2E completes and separately clean/reset its worktree

Requested default: Create an evidenced local checkpoint of the current e2e-test/ artifact, then repair in the same epic worktree
Requesting run: null
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.41

Approval preview:
- Action: Prepare an isolated safe write boundary for the approved DAG repair
- Scope: Either a local checkpoint of the existing e2e-test/ artifact or a separately approved new worktree; no source changes occur in this decision itself.
- Consequences: Allows the repair to proceed without weakening dirty-worktree safety or losing the current E2E artifact.
- Reversibility: A local checkpoint is reversible later only by separately approved scoped reset; new-worktree creation would likewise require separate cleanup approval.
- Closeout path: Record baseline/checkpoint or worktree evidence, then dispatch the already approved repair through the runner.
notes: Beads-backed approval resolved as answered: checkpoint — create an evidenced local checkpoint of the current e2e-test/ artifact, then run the approved DAG repair in the same epic worktree. No reset, merge, push, or E2E edits.
close_reason: Beads-backed approval resolved as answered: checkpoint — create an evidenced local checkpoint of the current e2e-test/ artifact, then run the approved DAG repair in the same epic worktree. No reset, merge, push, or E2E edits.

approval.answer: checkpoint — create an evidenced local checkpoint of the current e2e-test/ artifact, then run the approved DAG repair in the same epic worktree. No reset, merge, push, or E2E edits.

description:
Beads-backed approval request.

Question: Approve the durable dependency-DAG fix so planned workflows advance without chat “ok” wakeups?

Context:
Recommended simple design: expose native Beads dependencies when creating planned stages; permit only explicitly approved workflow implementation stages to auto-close after the existing evidence/readiness closeout succeeds; let the existing autonomous runner schedule each newly unblocked successor. This replaces main-chat completion observation as the control path. Default implementation tasks remain open; failures, empty output, blocked work, and unresolved approvals never unlock successors. Scope is gateway metadata/creation, scheduler close/advance behavior, gateway schema, focused tests, and runner docs—no E2E source edits, manual tick, repair mode, runner restart, push, merge, reset, branch/worktree deletion, hooks, or secrets access.

- Reject or revise design

Requesting run: unknown

title: After the interactive Pi/TUI process exits, should the project-local runner continue approved queued work?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: After the interactive Pi/TUI process exits, should the project-local runner continue approved queued work?

Context:
Pi supplies an async `session_shutdown` lifecycle event for normal quit/reload/session replacement. That is the correct clean-exit signal. A lease/heartbeat is needed only as fallback for unclean termination (kill -9, crash, power loss), but current source does not show the scheduler consulting lease expiry, so expiry is not yet proven as the dispatch-stall cause. The design choice determines whether clean TUI exit merely detaches the UI or drains/stops autonomous work. User previously asked that the orchestrator continue safe E2E work while they step away, which favors independent runner continuation.

Options:
- continue queued work after TUI exit; use session_shutdown to detach client and lease expiry only for stale-client cleanup
- drain/stop runner after clean TUI exit; do not schedule without live TUI
- continue active work but do not start new work after TUI exit

Requested default: continue queued work after TUI exit; use session_shutdown to detach client and lease expiry only for stale-client cleanup
Requesting run: runner-pi-2m1.27.23
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.24

Approval preview:
- Action: Set runner behavior across TUI process exit
- Scope: Lifecycle policy only; no source edits, service restart, dispatch, commit, reset, merge, or deployment.
- Consequences: Determines whether a clean TUI shutdown detaches a client while autonomous work continues, or drains the queue. It also bounds how process/liveness signals may be used.
- Reversibility: A future recorded decision can change the lifecycle policy; no runtime state changes occur now.
- Closeout path: Resolve policy, then revise the scheduler-liveness repair scope and tests to match the chosen behavior.
notes: Beads-backed approval resolved as answered: User agreed with the detached-runner/persistent-control-connection design. Policy: approved queued work continues after Pi/TUI exit; clean session shutdown detaches the client, while persistent-connection EOF and TTL are stale-client signals only and do not drain or cancel runner work.

title: What terminal response contract should all worker agents satisfy?
description:
Beads-backed question request.

Question: What terminal response contract should all worker agents satisfy?

Context:
E2E Stage 7 showed process exit code 0 with an empty response, leaving the orchestrator unable to infer task opinion or completion. This decision defines the universal worker output contract for runner closeout.

Options:
- allow empty response when exit code is 0
- require a non-empty terminal OK or ERROR result for every worker
- require a structured JSON-only terminal result

Requested default: require a non-empty terminal OK or ERROR result for every worker
Requesting run: runner-pi-2m1.27.20
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.22

Approval preview:
- Action: Set universal worker terminal-output policy
- Scope: Policy decision for empty-response closeout repair; no code changes or runner state changes.
- Consequences: Runner implementation will reject empty output and require agents to communicate an explicit completion/error opinion even when process exit is 0.
- Reversibility: Decision is durable and can be superseded later if a richer protocol is adopted.
- Closeout path: Resolve with user direction; cite from pi-2m1.27.22 acceptance, worker prompt contract, and regression tests.
notes: Beads-backed approval resolved as answered: User decision: every worker must emit a non-empty terminal response stating an explicit OK or ERROR outcome, even for trivial/no-op tasks. Include concise reason/evidence so the orchestrator can assess completion independently of process exit code. Empty output is invalid/unknown and must not be treated as success.
close_reason: Beads-backed approval resolved as answered: User decision: every worker must emit a non-empty terminal response stating an explicit OK or ERROR outcome, even for trivial/no-op tasks. Include concise reason/evidence so the orchestrator can assess completion independently of process exit code. Empty output is invalid/unknown and must not be treated as success.

title: Approve implementing the next E2E stabilization repair for verify-worker command execution plus tail-friendly worker logs/lessons artifacts?
description:
Beads-backed approval request.

Question: Approve implementing the next E2E stabilization repair for verify-worker command execution plus tail-friendly worker logs/lessons artifacts?

Context:
The Edabit E2E Stage 4 verify worker timed out after 300s. It lacked bash despite being required to run a read-only pytest command, and active-run observability lacked tail-friendly logs, lessons artifacts, and progress/status details. The user agreed this is a gap and asked to continue. This approval covers implementing pi-2m1.27.5 and pi-2m1.27.6 together in the current repair-oriented session.

Options:
- approved
- defer
- narrow-scope

Requested default: approved
Requesting run: unknown
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.5

Approval preview:
- Action: Edit runner/invocation tooling and tests to support safe read-only command execution for verify workers and stable live logs/lessons/handoff artifacts in run bundles.
- Scope: Allowed source scope: pi/agent/bin/agnt_lib/invoke.py, runs.py, runner.py, runner_client.py, runner_service.py, runner_scheduler.py and tests/test_agnt.py, tests/test_runner.py, tests/test_runner_client.py, tests/test_runner_service.py, tests/test_runner_scheduler.py. May update Beads/run evidence. No commit, push, merge, deploy, hooks, launch agents, branch/worktree deletion, secrets, or unrelated edits.
- Consequences: Should let future verify workers run non-mutating test commands and let the orchestrator inspect live worker state without bash/process workarounds. May change runner status schema and run bundle layout.
- Reversibility: Source changes can be reverted before commit; run evidence/Beads notes remain as audit trail. No destructive repository operations are included.
- Closeout path: RED/GREEN tests for verify command tool profile and live log artifact creation/status exposure; focused runner tests; project checks where appropriate; then retry a Stage 4-style verification smoke.

title: Approve checkpoint integration of Stage 2 e2e-test changes so Stage 3 can run?
description:
Beads-backed approval request.

Question: Approve checkpoint integration of Stage 2 e2e-test changes so Stage 3 can run?

Context:
The E2E workflow deliberately made Challenge B depend on Challenge A. Stage 2 completed in the epic worktree `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1` and left the expected uncommitted `e2e-test/` changes there. When Stage 3 was queued, the runner correctly refused to dispatch into the dirty epic worktree and created blocker `pi-ada` with reason `epic worktree is dirty`. This is a real workflow finding: dependent implementation stages need an explicit checkpoint integration or a designed continuation mechanism. To continue the E2E now, I propose to copy/merge the Stage 2 `e2e-test/` changes from the epic worktree into the main checkout, then clean only those `e2e-test/` changes from the epic worktree so the runner can dispatch Stage 3 cleanly. This does not commit, push, merge branches, delete worktrees, or rewrite history.

Options:
- approve-checkpoint-integration
- defer

Requested default: defer
Requesting run: unknown
Target bead: pi-2m1.27.2

Approval preview:
- Action: Checkpoint-integrate Stage 2 e2e-test files from `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1` into the main checkout, then remove the staged e2e-test working-tree changes from the epic worktree so Stage 3 can run.
- Scope: Only `e2e-test/edabit_challenges.py`, `e2e-test/test_edabit_challenges.py`, and `e2e-test/README.md` in the main checkout and epic worktree. No commit, push, branch merge, worktree deletion, Beads history rewrite, hook install, launch agent install, secrets access, or unrelated cleanup.
- Consequences: The main checkout will gain the Stage 2 e2e-test files as uncommitted changes; the epic worktree returns clean for the runner. This unblocks Stage 3 while preserving evidence of the worktree-policy gap.
- Reversibility: The main checkout additions remain uncommitted and can be removed later with approval; the epic worktree cleanup only removes the copied Stage 2 e2e-test changes after they are saved in main.
- Closeout path: Record the blocker and approval in Beads, verify Stage 2 tests pass in main, verify epic worktree clean, close/supersede `pi-ada`, and let the runner pick up Stage 3.
notes: Beads-backed approval resolved as approved: Approved by user in chat: “I approve pi-km7 and manual cleanup the dirty state from the epic worktree and continue to stage 3.” Scope remains checkpoint/manual cleanup of Stage 2 e2e-test state and continuation to Stage 3, with no push/merge/branch deletion/worktree deletion/history rewrite.
close_reason: Beads-backed approval resolved as approved: Approved by user in chat: “I approve pi-km7 and manual cleanup the dirty state from the epic worktree and continue to stage 3.” Scope remains checkpoint/manual cleanup of Stage 2 e2e-test state and continuation to Stage 3, with no push/merge/branch deletion/worktree deletion/history rewrite.

title: Approve cleanup of stale duplicate worker changes in epic worktree?
description:
Beads-backed approval request.

Question: Approve cleanup of stale duplicate worker changes in epic worktree?

Context:
During the pi-2m1.23 repair, the autonomous runner started a duplicate implementation worker in `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1`. That worker was later killed after hanging; it left uncommitted changes in the epic worktree (`pi/agent/bin/agnt_lib/runner.py`, `tests/test_runner.py`, and untracked `.pi/runner/`). The main checkout has the verified fix and live smoke passed, but `agnt work health --json --no-beads` and `scripts/check-pi-config.sh` still fail because the recorded epic worktree is dirty.

Options:
- approve-cleanup
- defer

Requested default: defer
Requesting run: unknown
Target bead: pi-2m1

Approval preview:
- Action: Discard stale duplicate-worker changes in `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1` and remove its untracked `.pi/runner/` runtime directory.
- Scope: Only the local epic worktree `.worktrees/epic/pi-2m1`; no main-checkout source edits, no commit, push, merge, branch/worktree deletion, Beads history rewrite, hooks, launch agents, or secrets access.
- Consequences: Project health checks should no longer fail on the dirty epic worktree. The killed worker's uncommitted alternate fix attempt will be discarded; the verified main checkout fix remains.
- Reversibility: Uncommitted worktree changes are not easily recoverable after reset/clean unless copied first; I can save a diff artifact before cleanup if desired.
- Closeout path: Save cleanup evidence, rerun work health/check-pi-config, and report PASS/remaining findings.
notes: Beads-backed approval resolved as cancelled: Superseded by approved cleanup decision pi-2m1.26; cleanup completed and verified.

title: Harden orchestrator-service startup shutdown race in headless RPC
description: Live acceptance for pi-2m1.11 showed the deployed orchestrator-service extension loading in Pi RPC mode, starting the runner, attaching a lease, and draining on shutdown, but the RPC lifecycle emitted session_shutdown before a stable non-draining running observation. The extension continued startup/polling after shutdown and later surfaced `orchestrator-service failed: fetch failed`. Harden session_start/session_shutdown ordering so startup awaits stop if shutdownStarted becomes true, polling is not started after shutdown, and expected already-drained shutdown does not surface a noisy fetch-failed widget.
acceptance_criteria: A regression test simulates shutdown during async startup and verifies no polling starts after shutdown, no fetch-failed widget is shown for expected drain/stop, lease/drain behavior remains idempotent, and normal session_start still starts/attaches/refreshes runner status.
close_reason: Hardened orchestrator-service startup/shutdown ordering so headless RPC shutdown aborts in-flight startup, prevents polling after shutdown, releases any late lease, requests drain idempotently, and suppresses expected already-stopped/fetch-failed shutdown errors.

title: Task 6: Add ticket gateway tool and plan-view command
description:
Why:
The main orchestrator needs a constrained control surface with no raw bash, raw Beads, raw subagent, edit, or write access.

What:
Add a ticket_gateway Pi extension/tool with strict enum operations and a /work or /plan-view command, exposing compact Beads/run context and approved recall tools only.

Plan section:
Task 6 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.
acceptance_criteria: Gateway/tool registers successfully, rejects free-form shell-like payloads, exposes only structured operations, and config checks pass.
notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 6
Starting Task 6 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: ticket gateway must expose strict structured operations and compact durable Beads/run context, not raw shell/Beads passthrough.
Task 6 evidence: implemented strict ticket gateway core in agnt_lib/gateway.py plus agnt gateway CLI and pi/agent/extensions/ticket-gateway.ts. Gateway supports enum operations list, show, tree, create_draft, request_approval, resolve_blocker, and runner_status. It rejects shell-like/raw command payloads recursively, whitelists fields per operation, emits compact Beads items with metadata validation and active run refs, delegates tree to the existing work-tree core, delegates request_approval/resolve_blocker to the Beads-backed approval core, and reserves a stable not_configured runner_status surface for Task 7. The extension registers ticket_gateway with StringEnum operations and a /work command; it delegates to agnt gateway and does not expose raw bash, raw bd, edit/write, or raw subagent. RED: tests/test_ticket_gateway.py initially failed 8 tests for missing ticket_gateway and missing extension. GREEN: tests/test_ticket_gateway.py passed 8; full tests/ passed 116. Config checks passed twice, bash -n scripts/*.sh passed, py_compile passed, ticket-gateway extension loaded with pi --no-extensions -e ... --list-models, agnt gateway runner_status smoke emitted valid JSON, shell-like payload rejection smoke returned exit 2 with expected error, routing-smoke and role-context-smoke evals passed, git diff --check passed. Peer review: gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini substantive PASS and metrics annotated.
close_reason: Implemented and verified strict ticket gateway core/CLI plus Pi extension and /work command.

title: Task 5: Implement Beads-backed ask and approval flow
description:

What:
Create ticket question/approval tooling or an ask bridge that records context, options, requesting run, answer/rejection/timeout, and blocking relationships in Beads and run results.

Plan section:
Task 5 in .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md
design: Follow the saved plan. Beads is canonical durable work state; Archimedes todos are ephemeral projections; worker sessions are recorded by default; observational memory is advisory recall/context and must be promoted into Beads or .pi/runs before affecting project state.

notes:
Plan: .pi/plans/2026-07-08-beads-first-autonomous-orchestration-plan.md; plan task: 5
Starting Task 5 under Phase 2 approval on main. Production-readiness constraint applies: Beads-backed ask/approval flow must create durable, auditable decision records and visible blockers, not a placeholder UI bridge.
Task 5 evidence: implemented Beads-backed ask/decision/approval core in agnt_lib/approvals.py with agnt approvals request/resolve. Requests create Beads decision issues with human/human-gate/beads-backed labels, informed-consent preview metadata (action, scope, consequences, reversibility, closeoutPath), context/options/default/requestingRun, and a blocking dependency via bd dep <decision> --blocks <target>. Requesting run results are updated to needs-human with decisionRefs and approvalRefs for approval gates. Resolution records approved/answered/rejected/cancelled/timed-out outcomes in Beads metadata/notes and run results; approved/answered closes the decision bead, while timeout/cancel/reject leave the blocker visible. Added thin Pi extension bridge pi/agent/extensions/beads-ask-bridge.ts registering ticket_question, ticket_approval, and ticket_decision_resolve; it delegates durable state to agnt approvals and creates the Beads blocker before optional UI confirmation. Added focused tests/test_approvals.py. RED: six approval tests failed for missing core functions and extension. GREEN: tests/test_approvals.py passed 6; tests/test_orchestration.py tests/test_approvals.py passed 19; full tests/ passed 108. Temporary Beads repo smoke created approval, blocker, resolved approved, and verified run refs/status. Extension load via pi --no-extensions -e beads-ask-bridge.ts --list-models passed. Config checks, bash -n, py_compile, git diff --check passed. Deterministic evals routing-smoke and role-context-smoke passed after aligning stale routing-smoke expected selected model with Task 3 policy output. Peer review: gemma4 and gpt-4.1-mini substantive PASS; qwen attempts rejected as stubs and metrics annotated.
close_reason: Implemented and verified Beads-backed ask/decision/approval flow with durable Beads blockers, run-result refs, and thin Pi bridge.

title: Decide Beads role configuration for pi-setup
description:
Why:
Repeated Beads commands in the production soak emitted: beads.role not configured (GH#2950). The warning adds noise to agent runs and may hide more important diagnostics.

What:
Decide whether this repository should set a repo-local beads.role value such as maintainer or contributor, document the decision, and apply it only if approved.

Acceptance criteria:
The decision is recorded; either the warning no longer appears in normal bd commands or the project documents why it is intentionally left unset.
notes: Decision: set repo-local git config beads.role=maintainer. Rationale: pi-setup is maintained directly in this checkout and the production soak Beads were created/owned by maintainer identities; maintainer matches the repository's maintenance role. Scope: local repository config only (.git/config), not a tracked secret or Beads remote/history change. Verification: git config --show-origin --get beads.role returned file:.git/config maintainer; bd ready no longer emitted the GH#2950 role warning; bd show pi-4lb --json returned valid JSON; agnt work health --json exited 0.
close_reason: Configured repo-local beads.role=maintainer and verified normal bd commands no longer emit the role warning.
